briefdescription.para: Start index in the N_ and colIndex_ vectors, of each row.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int rowNum, const vector< T > &entry, const vector< unsigned int > &colIndexArg)
memberdef.name: addRow

detaileddescription.para: Used to set an entire row of entries, already in sparse form. Assumes that the SparseMatrix has been suitably allocated. rowNum must be done in increasing order in successive calls.
Adds a row to the sparse matrix, must go strictly in row order. This is dangerous as there is a test for an empty entry ~0. For many types, ~0 may actually be a perfectly legal entry.

memberdef.definition: const vector< unsigned int > & SparseMatrix< T >::colIndex
memberdef.name: colIndex

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int row, unsigned int column) const
memberdef.name: get

detaileddescription.para: Returns the entry identified by row, column. Returns T(0) if not found

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int col, vector< T > &entry, vector< unsigned int > &rowIndex) const
memberdef.name: getColumn

detaileddescription.para: This is an unnatural lookup here, across the grain of the sparse matrix. Ideally should use copy_if, but the C++ chaps forgot it.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int row, const T **entry, const unsigned int **colIndex) const
memberdef.name: getRow

detaileddescription.para: Used to get an entire row of entries. Returns # entries. Passes back iterators for the row and for the column index.
Ideally I should provide a forall type function so that the user passes in their operation as a functor, and it is applied to the entire row.

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int row, vector< T > &e, vector< unsigned int > &c) const
memberdef.name: getRow

detaileddescription.para: getRow: vector version. Used to get an entire row of entries. Returns # entries.

briefdescription.para: Here we expose the sparse matrix for MOOSE use.

memberdef.argsstring: (const vector< unsigned int > &row, const vector< unsigned int > &col, T value)
memberdef.name: pairFill

detaileddescription.para: Prints out the contents in matrix form
Prints out the contents in internal form

Reorder columns from the matrix based on a map of old to new column indices. newCols contains the indices of columns from the old matrix that are to be retained, in the order that they will occupy in the new matrix. It can have fewer or more indices than the original.

memberdef.definition: const vector< unsigned int > & SparseMatrix< T >::rowStart
memberdef.name: rowStart

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned int row, unsigned int column, T value)
memberdef.name: set

detaileddescription.para: Assigns and if necessary adds an entry in the matrix. This variant does NOT remove any existing entry.
Should be called only at the start. Subsequent resizing destroys the contents.
Does a transpose, using as workspace a vector of size 3 N_ 0257 -> 0011122

memberdef.argsstring: (const vector< unsigned int > &row, const vector< unsigned int > &col, const vector< T > &z, bool retainSize=false)
memberdef.name: tripletFill
